stories.title: UK Finance Bill Draft Includes Oil Levy and HMRC Correction Power
stories.summary: The UK government published a draft Finance Bill on July 20, 2026 that includes provisions for a new oil and gas levy alongside a new HMRC correction power, giving tax officials enhanced tools to address errors in self-assessments.

stories.title: IRGC, IMCR and GRU Volunteer Corps Designated as National Security Threats
stories.summary: The UK formally designated Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), the IMCR, and Russia's GRU Volunteer Corps as threats to national security under the new National Security (State Threats) Act 2026. The designations took effect on July 17, giving the Home Secretary direct powers to act against these foreign state-backed organisations.

stories.title: New UK Immigration Act 2026 and Statement of Changes
stories.summary: The Immigration Act 2026 has come into force, alongside a Home Office Statement of Changes updating UK immigration rules. Key changes affect visa eligibility, entry requirements, and the Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) pathway.

stories.title: Nearly 70,000 Illegal Migrants Removed or Deported from UK
stories.summary: Home Office figures show almost 70,000 illegal migrants have been removed or deported from the UK, with illegal working arrests and raids reaching their highest level on record. However, more asylum seekers arrived from France under the cross-Channel deal than were returned.

stories.title: New UK PM Andy Burnham Takes Office Amid Middle East Escalation
stories.summary: Andy Burnham became UK Prime Minister on July 20, 2026, facing immediate foreign policy challenges including the ongoing US-Iran military conflict with US strikes entering a ninth night, rising oil prices above $90 a barrel, and significant global geopolitical pressures. Burnham has reportedly appointed Ed Miliband to a senior Foreign Office role.

stories.title: FCDO Updates Travel Advice for Multiple Countries
stories.summary: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office updated travel advisories for several countries including Spain, Italy, France, Cyprus and Uganda, reflecting the latest safety and entry requirement information for UK travellers.

stories.title: DWP Staff Stole £612,000 in Benefits Meant for Pensioners and Disabled Claimants
stories.summary: An investigation revealed that DWP staff stole over £612,000 in benefit payments intended for pensioners, disabled people and unemployed claimants during 2025/26. The department conducted 40 internal fraud investigations over the period.

stories.title: Housing Benefit to Remain with Local Authorities Under DWP Reforms
stories.summary: Weekly DWP news confirmed that Housing Benefit administration will remain with local authorities rather than moving to the DWP, while Pension Credit will continue to be administered by DWP. New rules are set to allow thousands of Housing Benefit claimants to retain more of their payments.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: Sustainable Farming Investment Scheme Opens 21 July 2026
stories.summary: DEFRA's reformed Sustainable Farming Investment (SFI) scheme opens for applications on July 21, 2026, with £5 billion committed over two years to help farmers manage land sustainably. The scheme pays farmers to improve soil health, biodiversity and environmental outcomes.

stories.title: £225 Million Capital Grants Round Opens for Farmers
stories.summary: A new round of DEFRA Capital Grants opened in July with £225 million available to help farmers and land managers invest in environmental improvements, including water management, hedgerow restoration and slurry infrastructure.

stories.title: Lib Dems Warn of 'Lost Generation of Farmers' as Youth Share Falls to 5%
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats unveiled a major food security plan warning that Britain risks a 'lost generation of farmers' as only 5% of farmers are now under 35. The plan calls for urgent DEFRA action on succession planning, training and farm access for young people.

stories.title: UK to Double Sky Sabre Air Defence Force With £350m+ Contract
stories.summary: Defence Minister Luke Pollard confirmed a £350 million-plus contract to double the UK's deployable Sky Sabre air defence force, adding six new CAMM launchers, command centres and support vehicles by 2027. The expansion significantly increases Britain's short-range air defence capability.

stories.title: Incoming PM Burnham Faces Urgent Defence Modernisation Challenges
stories.summary: Analysis highlights that new Prime Minister Andy Burnham faces two critical defence challenges: the poor state of military hardware across the armed forces, and the urgent need to modernise for future drone warfare. Observers suggest only increased spending can address the military's capability gaps.

stories.title: India–UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement Enters Into Force
stories.summary: The India–UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) officially entered into force on July 15, 2026, strengthening trade and investment ties between the two countries. DBT described it as a landmark deal improving market access and creating new opportunities for businesses and service providers.

stories.title: Chinese Company Demands Compensation Over British Steel Nationalisation
stories.summary: Chinese company Jingye Group demanded compensation from the UK government, claiming it is 'trampling on international investment rules' following the nationalisation of British Steel. The Department for Business and Trade acknowledged the demand and defended the government's decision.

stories.title: Washington Delegation Attends UK Airshow Seeking New Trade Deals
stories.summary: A Washington State trade delegation visited the UK airshow this week as part of a DBT-organised mini trade mission, seeking new bilateral deals in aerospace and advanced manufacturing. The visit is part of a longstanding UK-Washington trade agreement.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: Incoming PM Burnham Plans to Abolish DSIT, Sparking Tech Industry Backlash
stories.summary: Reports emerged that incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham plans to gut or abolish DSIT — the department leading Britain's AI and technology strategy — triggering fierce backlash from tech industry leaders. Senior figures including AI adviser Matt Clifford warned the move would be 'a big mistake', with Burnham labelled 'Luddite' by critics.

stories.title: UK Space Agency Publishes Seeds of Scale Accelerator Impact Report 2026
stories.summary: The UK Space Agency (part of DSIT) published its 'Seeds of Scale' accelerator impact report for 2026, highlighting advances made by space tech companies in agriculture, defence, maritime, climate and manufacturing through the agency's accelerator programme.

stories.title: Connectivity in Low Earth Orbit (C-LEO) Programme Call 3 Opens
stories.summary: The UK Space Agency (DSIT) launched the third call of its Connectivity in Low Earth Orbit (C-LEO) programme, seeking proposals to drive innovation in satellite communications, with funding of up to an undisclosed amount for successful applicants.

stories.title: NHS England Approves Cancer Drugs Offering Hope for Rare Blood Cancers
stories.summary: NHS England approved new cancer drugs on July 17, 2026, offering new hope for children and adults living with rare blood cancers. The approval follows NICE evaluation and will make the treatments available on the NHS for eligible patients.

stories.title: Improving UK Health to 2014 Levels Could Add 2% to GDP
stories.summary: A new report cited by The Guardian suggests that if Britain's population health were restored to 2014 levels, it could add approximately 2% to GDP — highlighting the economic case for NHS investment and preventative health strategies.

stories.title: NHS MenB Vaccine Drop-In Clinics Launched in Scotland
stories.summary: NHS Tayside launched MenB vaccine drop-in clinics from late July 2026, offering the meningitis B vaccine to eligible groups. Two doses are required for full protection and clinics are available for a limited time.

stories.title: Royal Mail Confirms Significant Changes to 2nd Class Delivery Service
stories.summary: Royal Mail has confirmed significant changes to its 2nd class delivery service across the UK in 2026, as part of broader postal reform. The changes affect delivery frequency and timelines, while maintaining the one-price-goes-anywhere service.

stories.title: Fraudster Ordered to Repay £5m to Royal Mail or Face Jail
stories.summary: A fraudster was ordered by a court to repay £5 million to Royal Mail or face a jail sentence. The case was linked to a major fraud that targeted the postal organisation.

stories.title: MPs Raise Concerns Over Widespread Royal Mail Delivery Delays
stories.summary: Several MPs raised concerns about widespread Royal Mail delivery failures, with letters arriving weeks late and deliveries being batched together. Royal Mail acknowledged there is 'more to do to improve reliability' following parliamentary pressure.
